_meta.description: Denver / Colorado Springs Metro — Manual URL Curation Results for scrape_registry ingestion
_meta.session_context: Step 3 equivalent of Portland metro blitz, applied to CO Front Range. Covers Denver Water service area, Colorado Springs Utilities, and all major suburban/special-district utilities. PWSIDs not yet resolved — Claude Code must look them up via SDWIS query before inserting.
target_areas: Denver-Aurora-Lakewood MSA (Denver, Adams, Arapahoe, Broomfield, Douglas, Jefferson counties)
co_context_notes: Colorado water provision is heavily fragmented. Denver Water is the dominant wholesale provider but dozens of independent special districts retail directly to customers.
Many suburban utilities are organized as Colorado Special Districts (CRS Title 32) with elected boards — they are NOT municipal departments. Each has its own PWSID.
Lakewood has NO municipal water utility — water is provided by ~12 different entities depending on address (Consolidated Mutual, Denver Water, Valley Water, etc.).
Rate cycles vary widely: Denver Water Jan 1; CSU Jan 1; TVWD Nov 1; Brighton Jan 1; some districts biennial.
Colorado Winter Quarter Average (WQA) or Average Winter Consumption (AWC) — typically Dec-Feb or Jan-Mar — is the standard basis for sewer rate calculations statewide.
Measurement units: most CO utilities bill in gallons (per 1,000 gal), unlike Pacific Northwest utilities that bill in CCF/HCF.
_meta.insert_sql_template: INSERT INTO utility.scrape_registry (pwsid, url, url_source, status, notes) VALUES (:pwsid, :url, 'curated_denver_cos', 'pending', 'Denver/CO Springs metro manual curation 2026-03-26') ON CONFLICT (pwsid, url) DO NOTHING;
_meta.pwsid_lookup_sql: SELECT s.pwsid, s.pws_name, s.city, s.state_code, s.population_served_count FROM utility.sdwis_systems s WHERE s.state_code = 'CO' AND s.pws_type_code = 'CWS' AND (UPPER(s.pws_name) LIKE :name_pattern OR UPPER(s.city) = :city) ORDER BY s.population_served_count DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 5;
_meta.pwsid_resolution_strategy: For each entry, query SDWIS using sdwis_name_hint and sdwis_city. CO special districts often appear under formal district names (e.g., 'CENTENNIAL WATER AND SANITATION DISTRICT') rather than colloquial names. Try both. Confirm population_served_count is plausible before inserting. Log unresolvable entries.
coverage_notes: 6 Denver core / Denver Water service-area utilities
7 West/northwest metro (Jefferson County: Arvada, Lakewood area, Wheat Ridge, Golden area)
6 South metro (Arapahoe/Douglas: Englewood, Littleton, Highlands Ranch, Parker, Castle Rock, Aurora)
2 Standalone suburban districts (Columbine WSD, Pueblo West)

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates effective Jan 1, 2025. Fixed monthly charge: $19.06/mo for 3/4" meter (most common). 3-tier volumetric structure (indoor essential / efficient outdoor / excess outdoor). Rates differ inside Denver vs. suburban distributor districts. Typical SFR using 104,000 gal/yr sees ~$2-$3/mo increase in 2025. Rates set annually in October by Board of Water Commissioners. 2026 rates also approved Oct 2025: fixed charge rises to $20.91/mo for 3/4" meter; tier 1 rate $3.02/ccf.
utilities.pwsid_note: Colorado's largest utility — serves Denver city plus ~84 suburban distributor districts wholesale. Suburban distributors each have their own PWSID and set local rates on top of Denver Water wholesale. This PWSID is for direct Denver Water retail customers. Suburban distributor PWSIDs are separate entries.

utilities.utility_name: South Adams County Water & Sanitation District
utilities.current_rate_summary: 7.5% increase in water and wastewater rates effective Jan 2025. Centralized water softening fee: $3.88/mo per ERU in 2025. Sewer flat rate for sewer-only/multi-family: $56.14/mo. AWC basis for sewer: Jan-Mar water use. Largest special district in Colorado. Buys treated water from Denver Water; also operates own treatment facility on alluvial and Arapahoe aquifer wells.
utilities.pwsid_note: Serves Commerce City and parts of unincorporated Adams County. Population ~61,000.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Current (2026) rate page confirmed. 2025 rates archived on same page. 4-tier residential structure (per 1,000 gal): Tier 1 (0-5,000 gal) / Tier 2 (5,001-10,000) / Tier 3 (10,001-19,999) / Tier 4 (20,000+). Separate irrigation meter rates. Sewer calculated from Winter Quarter Average (Dec-Feb). Historical rates (2022-2026) accessible from same page. PWSID CO0103005 confirmed from water quality report.
utilities.pwsid_note: PWSID confirmed from 2025 Water Quality Report. Third-largest water utility in Colorado. Sources: South Platte, Arkansas, and Colorado River basins.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2025 typical residential bill: ~$95.18/mo (water+sewer+stormwater at 6,000 gal use). Water rates consist of: per-1,000-gal consumption charge + administrative fee + capital improvement fee. 4.5% annual increases projected. Added $5/mo Drinking Water Loan Fee effective Nov 1, 2025 (BIL loan repayment). 2026 rates adopted Nov 17, 2025. Sewer based on winter water consumption (minimum per meter size).
utilities.pwsid_note: Englewood's rate page includes the best regional comparison chart in the metro — useful for cross-validation.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Littleton customers receive Denver Water for drinking water. Littleton sets and bills its own sewer rates. 2025 sewer rate increase: 5% for all customers. City of Littleton sewer page at littletongov.org. The Southwest Metropolitan Water & Sanitation District (SMWD) and Platte Canyon Water & Sanitation District also serve parts of Littleton — customers in those areas are billed separately. Water rate page URL needs direct confirmation.
utilities.pwsid_note: Water is supplied wholesale by Denver Water. Littleton's own PWSID may cover direct Littleton Water connections only. Check SDWIS carefully — also check Southwest Metro Water & Sanitation and Platte Canyon WSD as separate PWSIDs.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2025 rates page confirmed. Buys water from Denver Water and bills at Denver Water's suburban distributor rates ($19.06/mo fixed + volume tiers). Sewer rates set locally; 5% increase in 2025 per City of Littleton SPR rates. Also passes through Englewood outside-city sewer treatment rate of $4.58/1,000 gal effective Jan 1, 2025.
utilities.pwsid_note: Small special district in SW Jefferson County. Water is Denver Water wholesale. Rate page is a news article with current year rate summary — scraper should check annually for updated links.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 11% rate increase effective Jan 1, 2025 (Ordinance 3714, CD2024-254). Average residential winter bill: $39.59/mo; summer: $79.78/mo. Tiered water budget structure based on lot size for SFR. 2026 rates: another 11% increase effective Jan 1, 2026 (Ordinance passed Sep 23, 2025). ThorntonWater.com is the consumer-facing site; thorntonco.gov is official. Cost-of-service enterprise — rates cover only water, not growth expansion.
utilities.pwsid_note: Note: dual-domain situation. thorntonwater.com is public-facing; thorntonco.gov is the official billing/rates page. Both confirmed active.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 4% rate increase approved for 2025 and 2026. 2025 rates: SFR Tier 1 (1,000-8,000 gal) $4.20/1,000 gal in-city; Tier 2 (9,000-40,000) $7.66; Tier 3 (41,000+) $9.58. Meter service fee (5/8"): $15.34/mo in-city. Shaw Heights area: 1.10× multiplier; Out of city: 1.25× multiplier. Average resident cost increase: $3.47/mo winter, $6.14/mo summer. Annual rate schedule PDFs archived at westminsterco.gov/Archive.aspx?AMID=36. Standley Lake is Westminster's main source; also serves Northglenn and Thornton wholesale.
utilities.pwsid_note: Westminster manages Standley Lake and also provides wholesale water to Northglenn and Thornton as part of Standley Lake agreements.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates page confirmed with volume rate table. SFR: flat rate structure (all consumption same rate). Domestic use: $4.17/1,000 gal. Irrigation only: $7.27/1,000 gal. Sewer: annual charge = Average Water Consumption (Dec-Mar) × $7.40/mo; minimum fixed sewer charge $22.20/mo. Water source from Westminster/Standley Lake.
utilities.pwsid_note: Buys treated water from Westminster. Northglenn sets its own retail rates.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates effective Jan 1, 2025 confirmed on page. City provides water, wastewater, and storm drainage. Sewer based on winter average (Dec, Jan, Feb) water consumption; minimum 3,000 gal/mo. Rate tables on page for residential, multi-family, and commercial customers.

Jan 19, 2025 rate increase: fixed service charge +$3.42/mo for SFR; consumption charge +3%. Switched from bimonthly to monthly billing effective Jan 2025. Rate schedule approved Dec 9, 2025 for Feb 2026. Serves Wheat Ridge and Town of Mountain View. Denver Water wholesale purchaser.
utilities.pwsid_note: Established 1939. Serves Wheat Ridge and Mountain View. Rate page lists current schedule adopted Dec 2025 effective Feb 2026.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Valley Water District serves parts of Wheat Ridge and unincorporated Jefferson County adjacent to Arvada. Exact rate page URL not confirmed from search results — domain guessed from references. Scraper should attempt crawl from homepage.
utilities.pwsid_note: Small district referenced in Wheat Ridge provider listings. Rate URL not confirmed — flag for secondary verification.

utilities.utility_name: City of Arvada Water, Sewer and Stormwater
utilities.current_rate_summary: 10% water rate increase effective 2025; 14.5% wastewater; 13.75% stormwater (approved Oct 21, 2024). 2026 rates approved Oct 21, 2025. Bimonthly billing. Usage rate changes bimonthly based on water consumption. Annual rate increases planned per 10-year strategic plan (10% water/sewer, 5% stormwater). 2025 Meter Rates PDF available. Sources water from Denver Water's North System and Clear Creek.

New tiered rate system effective Feb 1, 2025. Monthly service charge by meter size + tiered volume rates per 1,000 gallons. Private non-profit stockholder-owned (mutual) company — not a municipal utility. Serves ~100,000 people in ~27 sq miles of Lakewood and parts of unincorporated Jefferson County. Sells capital stock at $2,600/share (tap fees). Note: Lakewood has ~12 different water providers depending on address — CMWC is the largest.
utilities.pwsid_note: Private non-profit mutual company — not a government entity. May appear in SDWIS under 'CONSOLIDATED MUTUAL WATER CO' or similar. Treat ownership_type as private/non-profit. Other Lakewood-area providers include Denver Water direct service, Valley Water, Jefferson County service districts — each would need their own entry.

utilities.current_rate_summary: City of Golden provides water to ~20,000 residents in Jefferson County. Rate page URL inferred from cityofgolden.net site structure — not directly confirmed from search results. Scraper should attempt crawl.
utilities.pwsid_note: Golden sources from Clear Creek. Rate page URL unconfirmed — flag for secondary verification.

utilities.utility_name: Platte Canyon Water and Sanitation District
utilities.current_rate_summary: Buys water from Denver Water and bills at Denver Water suburban distributor rates ($19.06/mo fixed + volume tiers for 2025). Local sewer rates set by District plus South Platte Renew (SPR) treatment charges from City of Littleton (5% increase 2025). Serves SW Jefferson County / Littleton area. Rate news pages updated annually with Denver Water and Littleton sewer rate info.
utilities.pwsid_note: Denver Water suburban distributor. Also serves as a relay for Littleton sewer rates info.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Denver Water suburban distributor serving SW metro Littleton area. 2025 Denver Water rates pass-through (fixed $19.06/mo for 3/4" meter + tiered volume). Littleton sewer rates pass-through (5% increase 2025). Rate news page updated annually confirming current year rates.
utilities.pwsid_note: Denver Water suburban distributor. Close overlap with Platte Canyon WSD service area — verify PWSID boundaries carefully.

utilities.utility_name: City of Aurora — Water Rates (duplicate note)
utilities.current_rate_summary: See id=3. Aurora is both a north/east Denver metro utility and a south metro utility — service area spans Arapahoe, Adams, and Douglas counties. Included here for regional completeness.
utilities.pwsid_note: Duplicate reference of id=3 for south-metro context. Do not insert twice — deduplicate before INSERT.

utilities.utility_name: Highlands Ranch Water (Centennial Water and Sanitation District)
utilities.current_rate_summary: Formerly known as Centennial Water and Sanitation District; rebranded to Highlands Ranch Water. 2025 rates effective Jan 2025 (bills from first cycle in 2025, ~Mar 4). Water rates: 11% variable rate increase; fixed charges held stable. Variable water rate: $5.32/1,000 gal within water budget (up from ~$4.79). Average annual SFR bill: ~$1,286 (2025) on 7,500 sq ft lot. Infrastructure Improvement Fee: $7.50/mo. Biennial billing (6 billing cycles/year). Wholesale water+wastewater provider to Highlands Ranch Metro District which retails to end customers.
utilities.pwsid_note: Complex structure: Highlands Ranch Water (formerly Centennial W&S) wholesales to Highlands Ranch Metro District and Mirabelle Metro District, which retail to end customers. PWSID may be under 'CENTENNIAL WATER AND SANITATION DISTRICT' in SDWIS. Also check 'HIGHLANDS RANCH METRO DISTRICT' separately.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2025 rate increases: water +1.82%, wastewater +1.47%, Long-Term Water Fee +$1.50/mo. Net increase: ~2.78% or ~$3.74/mo for typical residential customer. Well below regional average of ~5% for 2025. Mill levy reduced to 5.778 in 2025. Tiered water rate structure. Operates Rueter-Hess Water Purification Facility. Note: 5 other water districts also serve parts of Parker — PWSD does not cover all Parker addresses.
utilities.pwsid_note: Only one of ~6 water districts serving Parker. Other Parker-area providers include Pinery Water/Cherry Creek Valley, Stonegate Village, etc. — each would need separate entries.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Individualized water budget rate structure — each account has its own AWMC (Average Winter Monthly Consumption) for indoor budget and ET-based outdoor budget. Tier 1 (within budget): lower rate. Tier 2/3: higher rates for over-budget use. Rates reviewed annually. Castle Rock is among the most water-conservation-forward utilities in CO — reached 111 gpcd in 2025, one of lowest in nearly 2 decades. Rates are mid-range vs. SMWSA peers. Signed on to Platte Valley Water Partnership in Oct 2024.
utilities.pwsid_note: Listed as Town of Castle Rock in some sources. ColoradoScape rebate program notable context for rate structure.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Roxborough WSD has among the highest rates in the south metro — ~$193.45/mo per Englewood 2025 comparison (at 6,000 gal usage). This is a remote/rural area district with high capital costs. Exact current rate page URL not directly confirmed. Referenced in Englewood comparison data.
utilities.pwsid_note: URL inferred — not directly confirmed. Flag for secondary verification. Serves Roxborough Park area in Douglas County.

utilities.current_rate_summary: City Council approved 5-year rate case Nov 12, 2024. Water rates increase 6.5% annually 2025-2029. Wastewater 9%/yr. Electric 6.5%/yr. Natural gas 4%/yr. New rates effective Jan 1, 2025 (base rates); Energy Wise time-of-day electric rates effective Oct 1, 2025. 2025 budget: ~$1.8B (21% over 2024). Average residential customer projected +$80/mo total utilities by 2029. Community-owned quad utility (electric, natural gas, water, wastewater). Does not make a profit on rates. $3.9B infrastructure investment planned 2025-2029.
utilities.pwsid_note: CSU is a quad utility (electric, gas, water, wastewater) owned by the City of Colorado Springs. One of the largest municipally owned utilities in the US. The rates page at csu.org/rates covers all four services. Scraper should target water-specific tables.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rate page confirmed. Rates among lowest in Pikes Peak region — less than 1 cent per gallon. Unincorporated El Paso County community south of Colorado Springs. Groundwater from Widefield Aquifer (PFAS-treated). Also receives Fountain Valley Authority (FVA) surface water. 45% allocation of Venetucci wells.
utilities.pwsid_note: Serves Security community in unincorporated El Paso County. Distinct from Widefield Water and Sanitation District despite geographic proximity.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2025 rates page confirmed on wwsdonline.com. Serves unincorporated El Paso County (Widefield and Security communities) and City of Fountain. 45% share of Venetucci wells (Widefield Aquifer) plus Fountain Valley Authority surface water. PFAS treatment in place since 2017. 98% residential accounts.
utilities.pwsid_note: Serves Widefield community and parts of City of Fountain. Note that wwsdonline.com is Widefield W&SD; pueblowestmetro.us is Pueblo West — do not confuse.

utilities.current_rate_summary: City of Fountain provides water to ~30,000 residents south of Colorado Springs. Received 10% of Venetucci wells (Widefield Aquifer) PFAS-treated groundwater. Also receives water via Southern Delivery System (Arkansas River). Actively expanding water sources per 2025 news. Rate page URL inferred — not confirmed from search results.
utilities.pwsid_note: Rate page URL not confirmed — flag for secondary verification. City is actively pursuing new water sources in 2025-2026.

utilities.utility_name: Pueblo West Metropolitan District — Water & Sewer
utilities.current_rate_summary: RTS (Readiness to Serve) fee structure. 2025: new $1.50/mo residential service line repair program fee (effective Jan 2025, approved Nov 2024). 2026: typical household Water RTS fee $28.72/mo; Sewer RTS fee $41.48/mo. Utility fee schedule PDF available. Enterprise fund — no profit. Unincorporated community west of Pueblo city limits.
utilities.pwsid_note: Pueblo West is an unincorporated community in Pueblo County governed by a Metropolitan District. Not the same as Pueblo Board of Water Works (id=28).

utilities.current_rate_summary: Flat rate structure (all usage billed at same rate per 1,000 gal regardless of volume). Rates ~25% below Front Range average. Small steady annual increases 1.5-5%. Monthly billing. Jan 2026 rate information posted on customer service page. Sewer base period: Dec-Feb (Jan/Feb meter reads). Bill from Board of Water Works includes water, wastewater (City of Pueblo Wastewater Dept), and stormwater all on one bill. Exact 2025 rate PDF URL needs fresh fetch — the rate-insert PDF URL found reflects 2024 rates. Flag for secondary verification of 2025 rate amounts.
utilities.pwsid_note: Elected 5-member board. Unusual in CO: combined billing (water+wastewater+stormwater) but each function run by separate entity. Very low rates — one of lowest on Front Range. Rate insert PDF published annually ~Jan 1.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Already covered in id=27 (listed under colorado_springs region due to geographic ambiguity). Deduplicate before insert.

utilities.utility_name: Centennial Water and Sanitation District (historical name — use id=19)
utilities.current_rate_summary: Legacy name for Highlands Ranch Water (id=19). The district legally renamed itself but SDWIS may still list as Centennial Water and Sanitation District. Both names map to same PWSID. This entry exists to capture the old-name SDWIS lookup path.
utilities.pwsid_note: Alternate SDWIS name for Highlands Ranch Water (id=19). During PWSID lookup, try both names. Do not insert twice.

utilities.utility_name: Sterling Ranch Water — Dominion Water & Sanitation District
utilities.current_rate_summary: Sterling Ranch is a new master-planned community in Douglas County served by Dominion Water & Sanitation District. Among highest rates in region — ~$245.35/mo per Englewood 2025 comparison at 6,000 gal. High rates reflect new infrastructure capital costs. URL inferred — not confirmed.
utilities.pwsid_note: URL not confirmed — flag for secondary verification. Serves Sterling Ranch development. Very high rates reflect recent infrastructure investment.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Commerce City's water is provided by South Adams County Water & Sanitation District (id=2) for most of the city. The City of Commerce City itself may bill for sewer/stormwater separately. Rate page URL inferred from city website structure — not confirmed. Recommend checking SACWSD (id=2) as the primary water PWSID first.
utilities.pwsid_note: Water likely under South Adams County W&SD PWSID (id=2). Commerce City itself may only hold a sewer/stormwater PWSID or no separate water PWSID. Verify in SDWIS before inserting.

utilities.utility_name: City of Broomfield — Water and Wastewater
utilities.current_rate_summary: City and County of Broomfield operates its own water utility. Sources water from the Colorado-Big Thompson Project (CBT), South Platte, and groundwater. Rate page URL inferred from broomfield.org site structure — not confirmed from search results. Flag for secondary verification.
utilities.pwsid_note: Broomfield is both a city and county (unique in CO). Operates its own water utility independent of Denver Water.

utilities.utility_name: Cherry Creek Valley Water and Sanitation District
utilities.current_rate_summary: One of several water districts serving Parker area. Buys wholesale from Denver Water. URL inferred — not confirmed from search results. Flag for secondary verification.
utilities.pwsid_note: One of ~6 water providers in the Parker area. URL not confirmed — flag for secondary verification.

not_found_or_excluded.note: Serves Pinery area of Parker (Douglas County). One of 6 Parker-area providers. Rate page not searched within 5-minute budget. Recommend domain guesser or targeted search: 'Pinery Water District rates 2025'.
Another Parker-area provider. Small HOA-adjacent district. Rate page not searched.
Not to be confused with Centennial Water and Sanitation District (Highlands Ranch). Some north Thornton customers may be served by a different entity. Verify in SDWIS.
Serves Conifer/Evergreen area in Jefferson County mountains. Outside metro core. Not searched.
Rural Douglas County. Small system. No website likely — domain guesser candidate.
El Paso County north of Colorado Springs. Not searched within this session.

colorado_structural_notes: Denver Water wholesale vs. retail: Denver Water holds one PWSID for its direct retail service. Each of its ~84 suburban distributor districts holds a SEPARATE PWSID and appears separately in SDWIS. When inserting distributor district URLs, use the DISTRIBUTOR's PWSID, not Denver Water's. The distributor's rate page should be the URL (not denverwater.org).

Special District billing complexity: Many CO special districts bill water only, sewer only, or combined. Some water-only districts (like CMWC) don't bill for sewer at all. The scrape_registry entry should target the water rate data specifically.

Highlands Ranch dual-entity: Highlands Ranch Water (formerly Centennial W&S) is the wholesale/treatment entity; Highlands Ranch Metro District (HRMD) retails to end customers and collects the bill. HRMD does NOT have a PWSID — it's Highlands Ranch Water's PWSID that covers the population. The rate page at highlandsranchwater.org reflects what end customers pay.

Lakewood fragmentation: No single utility serves all of Lakewood. For SDWIS coverage, each provider (CMWC, Denver Water direct, Valley Water, etc.) holds its own PWSID. This session only confirmed CMWC (id=14). Other Lakewood providers need separate research.

Parker fragmentation: Parker Water & Sanitation District (id=20) covers only part of Parker. Other Parker-area PWS providers include Pinery Water, Cherry Creek Valley WSD, Stonegate, etc. Each is a separate PWSID.

Colorado measurement convention: Most CO utilities bill water in gallons (per 1,000 gallons), unlike Pacific Northwest utilities (CCF/HCF). When parsing scraped rate data, check unit labels carefully.
action_checklist: 1. Run SDWIS query for all CO CWS PWSIDs with population > 1,000 in Adams, Arapahoe, Broomfield, Denver, Douglas, El Paso, Jefferson, Pueblo counties.
2. For each entry in utilities[] where pwsid=null: query SDWIS using sdwis_name_hint AND sdwis_city. Try both formal district name and city/common name variants.
3. Skip entries with scraper_flag='DUPLICATE_SEE_ID_*' — deduplicate before insert.
4. For scraper_flag='NEEDS_VERIFICATION': insert URL anyway but flag in notes as 'url_unconfirmed' for post-scrape manual check.
5. For id=5 (Littleton): determine if Littleton holds its own water PWSID or if Denver Water covers it. Also look up Southwest Metro WSD and Platte Canyon WSD separately.
6. For id=14 (CMWC): look up PWSID under 'CONSOLIDATED MUTUAL WATER' in SDWIS. Private mutual company but still a CWS.
7. For Colorado Springs Utilities (id=23): CSU serves electric/gas/water/wastewater — scraper should target water-specific rate tables on csu.org/rates/.

9. Secondary research needed for: Broomfield (id=33), Golden (id=15), Fountain (id=26), Roxborough (id=22), Cherry Creek Valley (id=34), Valley Water District (id=12), Sterling Ranch/Dominion (id=31).

10. Pueblowater.org rate data confirmed as being there (Jan 2026 rate info on customer service page) but 2025 rate PDF URL stale — scraper should fetch pueblowater.org/customer-service/ and follow the 'January 2026 - Rate information' link to get fresh PDF.
